zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Dawnay於1495年出生在英國越可爾考維其可時，他的父親Sir Guy D’Awnay42歲，母親Lady Joan Darrell31歲。他於1514年5月29日與Dorothy Neville在英國越可爾石內卜 霍爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1552年3月10日在他的家鄉享年57歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁史乃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Dawnay于1495年出生在英国越可爾考維其可时，他的父亲Sir Guy D’Awnay42岁，母亲Lady Joan Darrell31岁。他于1514年5月29日与Dorothy Neville在英国越可爾石內卜 霍爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1552年3月10日在他的家乡享年57岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁史乃斯。

nameMeanings.name: Dawnay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from one of the many places in northern France named from an alder grove (Old French aulnai, aulnoi, latinized as alnetum), such as Annay (Pas-de-Calais), Aulnay (Aube, Marne, Calvados), Aulnois (Aisne), and Aunay (Calvados, Eure-et-Loire, Seine-et-Oise). With fused preposition, Anglo-Norman French d'Aunai became Dawnay, Dawney and (with loss of the final syllable) Dawn. Laney is another form of this surname with fused definite article. See also Dando , from Anglo-Norman French d'Aunou, with which d'Aunai may sometimes have been confused.

variants.name: Laney
meanings: Irish: probably a shortened and altered form of   McElhinney  .

English and Irish: habitational name introduced by the Normans originally deriving from any of the many places in northern France called L'Aunai (from Latin alnetum ‘alder grove’). It may also have developed to Delaney .
Variant of   Laine   2, a surname of French origin.

variants.name: Dawn
meanings: English (Derbyshire and Nottinghamshire): of Norman origin, probably a clipped form of Dawnay or Dawney (compare Dana ), habitational names from one of the many places in northern France named for an alder grove (Old French aulnai, aulnoi, Latinized as alnetum). With fused preposition d(e), Anglo-Norman d'Aunai became Dawnay, Dawney and (with loss of the final syllable) Dawn. Laney is another form of this surname with fused definite article. See also Dando , from Anglo-Norman d'Aunou, with which d'Aunai may sometimes have been confused.

English: from Middle English Dawen, a variant of Dawes with the weak declension genitival suffix -(e)n (Old English -an) instead of the standard Middle English -es. The usage is mainly restricted to West Midlands dialects of Middle English.

variants.name: Doney
meanings: English (Cornwall): of uncertain origin. There is a Richard Donowe recorded in 1451-64 as a tenant in Manuels, Saint Columb Minor, whose surname appears to be a pet form of an unidentified personal name with the Cornish hypocoristic suffix -ow. Formally this might have become Doney but Donowe itself seems not to have survived, unless it was altered to Downey . However, there is no evidence to suggest any connection of this west Cornwall surname with Doney in central and east Cornwall (Bodmin and Quethiock). Alternatively, perhaps it is a variant of the Devon name Downey , and with the main vowel shortened and lowered, but evidence for this is lacking.
Americanized form of French Canadian   Daunais   or   Donais  .

variants.name: Dando
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name for someone from Aunou-le-Faucon in Orne, Normandy, with fused preposition. Aunou was named for an alder grove, from Latin alnus ‘alder’ + the Gaulish suffix -avum. In medieval England d'Aunou developed an intrusive -d-, giving Dando. The surname was sometimes confused with d'Aunay or d'Auney (see Delaney ).

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Dawnay was born in 1495, in Cowick, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir Guy D’Awnay, was 42 and his mother, Lady Joan Darrell, was 31. He married Dorothy Neville on 29 May 1514, in Snape Hall, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 10 March 1552, in his hometown, at the age of 57, and was buried in Snaith, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Dorothy Neville le 29 mai 1514, à Snape Hall, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 10 mars 1552, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Snaith, Yorkshire East Riding, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.St. Laurence Priory Churchyard, Snaith,: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 스나이스
ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Dawnay(이)가 1495년 영국 요르크실르 코윅에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Guy D’Awnay(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Joan Darrell(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1514년 5월 29일 영국 요르크실르 스나프 홀에서 Dorothy Neville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1552년 3월 10일 그의 고향에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 스나이스에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Dorothy Neville em 29 de maio de 1514, em Snape Hall, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.St. Laurence Priory Churchyard, Snaith,: снеит, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Dawnay родился в 1495 г. в местности коуикк, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Guy D’Awnay было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Joan Darrell, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Dorothy Neville 29 мая 1514 г. в местности снап холл, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 10 марта 1552 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности снеит, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания.
